name: 37 · Internal Model Pool Agent
description: Model-pool P1 (docs/28 + docs/29) — an Agent step bound to a self-hosted model that is served through the in-cluster inference router, never net-admitted. The Agent runs a SINGLE-SHOT degenerate loop (no tools, maxTurns 1) so it deploys on the plain dev executor; the inference HTTP call goes `base_url → router` via the bound `internal_pool_router` (`internal_llm`) resource, which is the whole point of the model-pool seam (inference bypasses the engine Petri net + the presence net). The model id `llama3.2:1b` (small + fast so the degenerate inference completes inside the template-test's 60s wall clock on a CPU-only box; pulled by `just dev::up-ollama` alongside the main model) is curated in the `internal_pool_registry` (`model_registry`) approved SET and seeded `loaded` in `model_states`, so `GET /api/v1/models` reports it loaded and the editor's internal-pool Model picker offers it. The compiled wire provider is `openai` (OpenAI-compatible router path — the executor `Provider` enum knows only openai/anthropic/ollama; `internal` is the FE-only routing signal that selects the `internal_llm` resource type and locks per-step base_url/api_key overrides for GDPR). Live e2e: the fixture's `base_url` is the OpenAI-compatible Ollama shim host (`http://localhost:11434` — the LLM backend appends `/v1/chat/completions`) as the buildable fallback for a plain `just dev` stack; repoint it at the Router-MVP (`http://localhost:13200`) once that is up. The shim is unauthenticated, so `internal_pool_router` carries no `api_key` (the LLM backend sends no `Authorization` header when none is staged). Requires `just dev::up-ollama` with the default model (or change `data.model.model` + the registry/model_states fixtures to match `OLLAMA_MODEL`).
